title: Macro Distance Model for Sector Rotation
text: Same methodoloty as factor timing, but on sectors.

title: Macro Distance Model for Sector Rotation
text:
Methodology

7 financial state variables (oil, copper, vix, yield curve, monetary policy, market, stock-bond correlation)
Normalise each variable via rolling z-score (10-year window), winsorised at +/-3
Euclidean distance between current month and all historical months
Exclude last 36 months from similarity calculation to avioid momentum loading
Forecast factor returns using similarity-weighted historical averages
Backtest against Fama-French 6 factors + Momentum

GLD
Gold


TLT
LT Bond




This version forecasts long-only sector excess returns (vs equal-weighted benchmark), producing a sector rotation signal rather than a factor tilt signal.

title: Macro Distance Model for Sector Rotation
text:
3. Compute Excess Returns
For sector rotation, the relevant signal is excess return vs the equal-weighted sector benchmark. E.g. A sector that returns 5% when the average sector returns 8% underperformed.
We compute: \[r^{xs}_{i,t} = r_{i,t} - \bar{r}_t\]
where \(\bar{r}_t\) is the cross-sectional mean return across all available sectors at time \(t\).


Mean absolute row sum: 0.000000

Common date range: 2008-07-31 to 2026-04-30
Common months: 214

State variables retained: 11:
   oil            : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   copper         : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   vix            : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   spx            : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   credit_spread  : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   us_10y         : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   us_3m          : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   real_rates     : 0.9299065420560748 obs, 7.0% missing
   yield_curve    : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing
   stock_bond_corr: 0.9439252336448598 obs, 5.6% missing
   dispersion     : 1.0 obs, 0.0% missing

Forward fill missing (up to 3 months)...

title: Macro Distance Model for Sector Rotation
text:
3. Fit Distance Model
Implement regime similarity model: - Rolling 10 year z-score normalization (min 2 year) - Winsorisation at +/-3 - 36 month exclusion window - Euclidean distance metric - Similarity weights = 1 - d/d_max, then squared to emphsise closest match - NaN imputed with cumulative column means


Distance model fitted: 153 valid periods out of 214
Variables: ['oil', 'copper', 'vix', 'yield_curve', 'spx', 'stock_bond_corr', 'us_3m']

title: Macro Distance Model for Sector Rotation
text:
5. Sector Rotation Backtest
Three approaches 1. Top-K rotation: Each month, overweight the K sectors with the highest similarity-weighted forecast excess return, equal-weight the rest at zero. Pure long-only rotation. 2. Long-short rotation: Long top K, short bottom K sectors (dollar neutral). 3. Continuous weights: Use similarity-weighted average returns to forecast, then scale by forecast variability


Sector forecasts generated: (153, 13)
Date range: 2013-08-31 to 2026-04-30

title: Macro Distance Model for Factor Timing
text: Replication and extension of Man Group’s regime-based similarity model

title: Macro Distance Model for Factor Timing
text:
Methodology

7 financial state variables (oil, copper, vix, yield curve, monetary policy, market, stock-bond correlation)
Normalise each variable via rolling z-score (10-year window), winsorised at +/-3
Euclidean distance between current month and all historical months
Exclude last 36 months from similarity calculation to avioid momentum loading
Forecast factor returns using similarity-weighted historical averages
Backtest against Fama-French 6 factors + Momentum


Man group benchmark

Q1-Q5 spread portfolio sharpe: 0.82
Correlation to static long-only: 0.37

title: Macro Distance Model for Factor Timing
text:
5. Factor Timing Backtest
Two approaches 1. Continuous weights: Use similarity-weighted average returns to forecast, then scale by forecast variability 2. Quintile approach: Classify historical dates into quintiles by similarity, go long factors with positive avg return in Q1, short if negative

title: Welcome
text:
Hey there
I’m Vivian - quant researcher by day, curious tinkerer by night
I spend most of my time building systematic strategies and hunting for signals in noisy data. This is where I share the stuff I find interesting along the way - Research notebooks - Half-baked ideas - Occasional deep dive
Expect a mix of: - Factor research and alpha ideas - Playing with alternative data - Things that made me go hmm that’s weird
Nothing here is investment advice - just me poking at data and seeing what falls out.
